title: scenario_arson_serial_events
description: A jurisdiction experiences a series of deliberately set fires over several weeks, stretching resources and requiring coordinated investigation and prevention efforts.

timeline.title: Third Fire in Two Weeks
timeline.description: A third suspicious structure fire occurs within a four-block radius in 14 days. A serial arsonist is suspected.

timeline.title: Pattern Analysis Initiated
timeline.description: Fire marshal and law enforcement analyze ignition locations, times, and methods for a behavioral pattern.

timeline.title: Task Force Formed
timeline.description: An arson task force is assembled including fire investigators, police detectives, and a behavioral analyst.

timeline.title: Fourth Fire Occurs
timeline.description: A fourth fire is set before the task force can identify a suspect. Community concern escalates.

timeline.title: Surveillance Deployed
timeline.description: Covert camera systems placed at high-risk locations based on pattern analysis.

timeline.title: Suspect Identified and Apprehended
timeline.description: Surveillance footage leads to a suspect identification. Subject arrested. No further fires occur.

node-1.title: Third Fire in the Area
node-1.situation: A third fire has been set in an abandoned commercial block over 14 days. All fires occurred between 0200 and 0400. Each used a similar ignition method at a rear entry point. The fire marshal requests your input on operational response and coordination.
node-1.question: What is your immediate priority when a serial arsonist pattern is confirmed?

options.label: A
options.text: Formally declare a serial arson event, notify law enforcement leadership, establish a joint task force, and brief all field personnel on scene awareness indicators.

options.label: B
options.text: Treat each fire independently and wait for law enforcement to establish a pattern before changing operations.

options.label: C
options.text: Increase suppression resources only and do not adjust community communication strategy.

node-2.title: Task Force Strategy Meeting
node-2.situation: The arson task force meets to develop a prevention and detection strategy. A behavioral analyst suggests the arsonist is likely local, moves on foot, and targets properties with concealed rear access. The group must decide how to deploy limited surveillance and patrol resources.
node-2.question: How do you allocate resources for serial arson prevention?

options.label: A
options.text: Use pattern analysis to identify the highest-risk properties matching the profile, deploy covert cameras and plainclothes patrols at those sites, and conduct community outreach for tips.

options.label: B
options.text: Flood the entire area with marked patrol units around the clock regardless of risk profiling.

options.label: C
options.text: Wait for a fifth fire to gather more data before deploying surveillance.

node-3.title: Fourth Fire Occurs Before Disruption
node-3.situation: Despite initial task force efforts, a fourth fire is set at a location not covered by surveillance. Community outrage grows. Media is reporting that authorities have failed to stop the arsonist. Leadership requests a public statement.
node-3.question: How do you manage public communication during an active serial arson investigation?

options.label: A
options.text: Issue a factual public statement acknowledging the ongoing investigation, the task force's efforts, and request community tips through a tip line — without disclosing investigative details.

options.label: B
options.text: Release all investigative details including the suspect profile to reassure the public.

options.label: C
options.text: Make no public statement to avoid alerting the suspect.

node-4.title: Tip Line Yields Suspect Lead
node-4.situation: An anonymous tip describes a person seen near two of the fire sites who matches the behavioral profile. Investigators want to surveil the individual, but need a warrant to access a security camera on private property that may have captured additional activity.
node-4.question: How do you proceed with the investigative lead?

options.label: A
options.text: Pursue the warrant through proper legal channels, continue covert surveillance of the suspect from public areas, and brief the arrest team on safety protocols given the arson risk.

options.label: B
options.text: Access the private camera without a warrant to save time.

node-5.title: Scenario Complete
node-5.situation: The warrant was obtained, the private camera footage confirmed the suspect's identity, and the individual was apprehended without incident. No further fires have been reported. The task force approach — combining pattern analysis, targeted surveillance, and community engagement — successfully resolved the serial arson series.
node-5.question: Scenario complete. Well done!
